india_trends.title: India extends financial support to Maldives under SAARC
india_trends.summary: India has approved a significant ₹3,000-crore currency swap for the Maldives. This move aims to bolster the island nation's economy under the SAARC framework. The facility will help the Maldives manage foreign exchange needs and economic pressures. This support underscores the strong partnership bet

india_trends.title: Treasuries Fall as Oil Rally Boosts Inflation Expectations
india_trends.summary: Treasuries fell, lifting yields to the highest levels in several weeks, as rebounding oil prices drove up inflation expectations and curbed expectations for Federal Reserve interest-rate cuts.

india_trends.title: I Squared Cap to exit Polaris Smart Metering
india_trends.summary: I Squared Capital is looking to sell its stake in Polaris Smart Metering. The US-based fund has hired an investment bank to manage the sale. Polaris Smart Metering is valued at around $200 million. The company has secured contracts for smart meter installations in Uttar Pradesh and West Bengal. Pola

india_trends.title: Lenders turn to AI for collections and customer acquisitions
india_trends.summary: Indian lenders are turning to artificial intelligence to manage loan collections and acquire new customers. This move comes as a severe heatwave is expected across India. Cash-based collections and on-ground visits for loan repayments are particularly vulnerable to disruptions. Lenders are also tigh

india_trends.summary: Sensex, Nifty close the day in red amid FII selling and elevated crude oil prices    India's News.Net

india_trends.title: IndusInd is under lens over recovery incident
india_trends.summary: IndusInd Bank executives are now facing inquiries from law enforcement as two separate incidents of alleged assault by recovery agents over credit card debts come to light. Amid growing concerns, the Reserve Bank of India demands clarification on the bank's collection practices. Investigations into

india_trends.title: MFIs bounce back after 11 quaters of contraction and write-offs
india_trends.summary: India's microfinance sector showed a strong rebound. The loan portfolio expanded significantly in the March quarter. This marked a recovery after a long period of contraction and write-offs. Delinquency rates had also declined. This positive trend is expected to continue.

india_trends.title: Financial inclusion 2.0 road map set for review this week
india_trends.summary: This week, the Finance Ministry is set to evaluate the advancements in financial inclusion, laying the groundwork for Financial Inclusion 2.0. Officials will scrutinize existing initiatives such as the Atal Pension Yojana.

india_trends.title: UAE, Saudi Arabia bypass Hormuz to boost crude supplies to India
india_trends.summary: India’s crude imports fell as West Asia disruptions hit supplies, with average intake dropping to 4.4 million barrels per day in April 1–26, about 15% below February levels. However, steady flows from Saudi Arabia and higher shipments from the UAE helped offset some of the shortfall, alongside renew

india_trends.title: Oaktree Capital Accuses BJ’s of Reneging on Tariff Refund Trade
india_trends.summary: Oaktree Capital Management LP sued BJ’s Wholesale Club Inc. for allegedly backing out of a deal to sell its rights to around $29 million in tariff refunds for 70 cents on the dollar.

india_trends.title: Sebi offers a way over FPI tax rule hurdle
india_trends.summary: In a bid to streamline tax compliance, India's markets regulator Sebi is actively negotiating with the CBDT, the country’s tax authority. The proposal on the table suggests that foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) should have the autonomy to designate authorized signatories.

india_trends.title: OxyContin maker Purdue Pharmas criminal sentence could be the last step before company dissolves
india_trends.summary: OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma's criminal sentence could be the last step before company dissolves

india_trends.title: Ferrari eyes faster India expansion on back of EU-India FTA boost
india_trends.summary: Ferrari anticipates accelerated growth in India, driven by a significant reduction in import duties following the EU FTA. The company is encouraging early orders, with customers expected to receive vehicles at a lower price within 18 months. This strategic move aligns with Ferrari's dedicated focus

india_trends.title: Inspection scheme soon to flag firms dodging EPFO
india_trends.summary: In a bid to strengthen worker protections, the Labour and Employment Ministry is launching a comprehensive inspection program. This proactive approach will target businesses that fail to register with the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation.

india_trends.title: India fast-tracks Canada FTA, GCC talks paused, says Piyush Goyal
india_trends.summary: India is pushing forward with trade deals. Negotiations for a free trade agreement with Canada are speeding up. Talks are also underway with Chile and Peru. However, discussions with the Gulf Cooperation Council have been temporarily halted due to the ongoing conflict in West Asia. India plans to wa

india_trends.title: India industrial output at 4.1%, hit by Iran war
india_trends.summary: India's industrial output grew 4.1% in March, a five-month low. This slowdown was influenced by weaker electricity and manufacturing sectors. Economists noted the impact of the Iran conflict on supplies and costs. Despite the moderation, growth surpassed expectations. Manufacturing saw a dip, though

india_trends.title: India weighs induction cooktop subsidy amid Iran war energy crisis
india_trends.summary: India is exploring incentives for induction cooktops. This move aims to transition households from LPG to solar electricity. The government is discussing support under the PM Suryaghar Muft Bijli Yojana. This initiative seeks to boost the adoption of electric cooking. Tax cuts and reduced import dut

india_trends.title: Visiting Canada next month to speed up FTA negotiations: Piyush Goyal
india_trends.summary: Minister Piyush Goyal will travel to Canada next month to accelerate free trade agreement talks. India and Canada aim to finalize the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement soon. Discussions are also ongoing with Chile and Peru. Trade pacts with Gulf Cooperation Council nations are proceeding,

india_trends.title: Centre says 'no' to Andhra's request for diesel supplies at retail prices for Amaravati
india_trends.summary: The Centre has rejected Andhra Pradesh's plea to supply diesel at retail prices for Amaravati's construction. Union Petroleum Secretary Neeraj Mittal stated the request violates petroleum rules. He urged the state to withdraw the directive, emphasizing that retail outlets are not licensed for bulk s

india_trends.title: CCI clears Mizuho Securities stake purchase in Avendus Capital
india_trends.summary: The regulator also cleared the merger of agri firms into BN Agrochem Ltd and approved investments by Macquarie Group Ltd’s infrastructure fund in road asset entities.
